description: Lead Depot agent coaching tips. Add, edit, or reorder freely. Each tip needs: id, category, type, text, and optional author.

tips.text: Time-block your calls. 45 minutes on, 15 off. You'll dial more and burn out less.
Before you dial, silence everything else. One tab, one lead, one conversation at a time.
Set a dial goal before you open Lead Depot. 20 dials. 30 dials. Whatever it is — commit to it first.
The secret of getting ahead is getting started.
tips.author: Mark Twain

tips.text: Every no is one call closer to the yes. The math is always working in your favor.
You are not interrupting their day — you are offering a solution to a problem they haven't solved yet.
Success is the sum of small efforts repeated day in and day out.
tips.author: Robert Collier

tips.text: A tough morning session doesn't define your day. Reset between blocks. Shake it off and dial again.
If they hesitate, ask: 'What would need to be true for this to make sense for you?' Then listen.
Use LPMAMAB as a checklist, not a script. The goal is a real conversation, not a perfect read-through.
Lead with curiosity, not urgency. Ask about their timeline before you pitch yours.
The best appointment-setters talk less and listen more. Ask a question, then go quiet.
When you reach voicemail, leave a confident 15-second message. Your name, one sentence of value, your number — twice.
Need a roleplay? Text the group — someone's always down to practice. Ten minutes of roleplay beats a hundred cold calls.
Group call hours are there for a reason. There's energy in dialing together that you can't manufacture alone.
If you're stuck on an objection, bring it to training. Chances are everyone else is hearing it too.
Alone we can do so little; together we can do so much.
tips.author: Helen Keller

tips.text: You can't pour from an empty cup. Hydrate, move your body, and dial.
Take your breaks. A rested agent converts better than a burned-out one every single time.
Celebrate the small wins. A KIT is a win. A real conversation is a win. Track them — they add up.
